summary: Backend-only testing for newly added Admin Panel (JWT auth + CRUD). Created /app/backend/tests/test_admin.py with 38 tests covering login, /auth/me, auth guards on every admin endpoint (auth required + bad-token reject), full CRUD lifecycles (create/update/update-404/delete/delete-404) for research-focus, publications, lab-members, news, lab-info update with persistence verification, and contact-submissions list (desc-sorted) + delete. All 38 admin tests passed. Full suite: 56/56 passed (100%).

minor.issue: Uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') instead of FastAPI lifespan context manager. Functional but emits deprecation warnings.
Admin role-check error returns 401 'Not authorized' instead of 403. Semantically should be 403 Forbidden when authenticated but lacking role. Low priority since only one admin role exists today.

action_items: Optional: migrate startup/shutdown handlers to FastAPI lifespan handler.
Optional: return 403 (not 401) when token is valid but role != admin to better reflect HTTP semantics.
Optional: response_model on DELETE admin endpoints currently returns plain dict; consider a typed SuccessResponse.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT auth correctly implemented: HS256, 24h expiry, token type=access; rejects expired and invalid tokens with 401.

Password hashing uses bcrypt with $2b$ format; verify_password used on login and on startup admin password drift detection. Seed admin logic is idempotent and self-heals password changes via env vars.
Email is lowercased on login lookup matching seeded admin (case-insensitive verified).
All admin responses correctly project out _id; Pydantic response_models enforce shape.
get_next_id queries collection sorted desc by id - works but susceptible to race conditions under concurrent writes (not an issue for single-admin panel).

CORS allow_origins falls back to '*' but allow_credentials=True is set together with '*' which browsers reject for credentialed requests. With Bearer-in-header auth (not cookies) this is fine; tighten origins only if cookie auth is added later.
JWT_SECRET defaults to 'change-me-in-production' if env missing - good that .env sets a strong secret; consider failing-fast if unset in production.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /auth/login (playbook recommends lockout after 5 fails). Acceptable for an internal lab admin panel but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed records (research focus, publication, lab member, news, contact submission) and clean them up via DELETE within the same test. Lab info tagline is mutated and restored to original value.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin panel backend is fully functional. Auth = Bearer JWT in Authorization header (not cookies). Seeded admin self-heals password from ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars on every startup. Run full suite: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. All endpoints listed in review_request verified passing. No regressions in existing public endpoints.
